THE WINDSHIELD CRACKED WITHOUT A REASON WHILE I WAS DRIVING. THE CRACK STARTS AT THE TOP, APPROXIMATELY AT THE CENTER.  IT STARTED TO EXPAND DOWN AND IN A FEW HOURS HAS REACHED HALF OF THE HEIGHT. I CHECKED WITH FEW REPAIR SHOPS AND THE VERDICT IS THE WINDSHIELD NEEDS A REPLACEME

THE CAR HAS A FAULTY ELECTRONIC SYSTEM THAT CONTROLS THE STABILITY AND THE ADAPTIVE CRUISE CONTROL. TRANSMISSION ALSO NEEDS REPLACEMENT. PROBLEMS HAPPEN AT HIGH SPEED ON FREEWAYS. DOCUMENTED THE PROBLEM WITH VARIOUS YOUTUBE FOOTAGE AS FOLLOWS:    HTTPS://WWW.YOUTUBE.COM/WATCH?V=O

narrative.headline: 2020 Subaru Outback: windshield and wiper complaints lead by far
narrative.summary: Windshield cracking is the dominant complaint pattern for this model, reported both as spontaneous fractures and cracks originating from the windshield edge or center that expand over time. Electrical system faults make up the next largest share, with owners describing unexpected engagement of the electronic emergency brake while driving. Forward collision avoidance issues and additional windshield-related visibility complaints round out the most-reported problem areas.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: Owners report windshield cracks forming spontaneously, typically originating from the edge on the passenger side and measuring several inches in length.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the electronic emergency brake engaging without warning while the vehicle is in motion, such as when accelerating from an intersection. · 3 crash

issues.title: Visibility
issues.blurb: Owners report discovering windshield fractures on the lower passenger side, sometimes noticed after starting the vehicle, with cracks appearing below the wiper blade area. · 1 crash

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Owners report windshield cracks appearing without apparent cause while driving, starting near the top center and expanding downward over the course of hours. · 4 crash

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 45 reports (5%), tied to 2 crashes. No fires or injuries reported.
